structs.description:
An oscillator that generates a ramp waveform from 0.0 to 1.0. The phasor is the root of all oscillators in MMMAudio.

The Phasor can act as a simple phasor with the .next() function. 

However, it can also be an impulse with next_impulse() and a boolean impulse with next_bool().

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.summary: Args:     world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

args.description: Frequency of the phasor in Hz.
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator.
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True.
args.name: trig

overloads.name: next

returns.doc: The next sample of the phasor output.

overloads.signature: next(mut self: Phasor[num_chans, os_index], freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans] = SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans](True)) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Creates the next sample of the phasor output based on the inputs.

args.description: Frequency of the phasor in Hz (default is 100.0).
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is all True, which resets the phasor on the first sample).
args.name: trig

overloads.name: next_bool

returns.doc: A boolean SIMD indicating True when the impulse occurs.

overloads.signature: next_bool(mut self, freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans] = SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans](True)) -> SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Increments the phasor and returns a boolean impulse when the phase wraps around from 1.0 to 0.0. This only works with possive frequencies and phase offsets between 0.0 and 1.0.

args.description: Frequency of the phasor in Hz (default is 100.0).
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is all True, which resets the phasor on the first sample).
args.name: trig

overloads.name: next_impulse

returns.doc: The next impulse sample as a Float64. 1.0 when the impulse occurs, 0.0 otherwise.

overloads.signature: next_impulse(mut self, freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans] = SIMD[DType.bool, num_chans](True)) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Generates an impulse waveform where the output is 1.0 for one sample when the phase wraps around from 1.0 to 0.0, and 0.0 otherwise. This only works with possive frequencies and phase offsets between 0.0 and 1.0.

structs.name: Phasor

parameters.description: Oversampling index (0 = no oversampling, 1 = 2x, up to 4 = 16x). Phasor does not downsample its output, so oversampling is only useful when used as part of other oversampled oscillators.
parameters.name: os_index

structs.description:
An oscillator that outputs a 1.0 or True for one sample when the phase wraps around from 1.0 to 0.0.

Impulse is essentially a wrapper around the Phasor oscillator that provides impulse-specific methods.

structs.description:
A wavetable oscillator capable of all standard waveforms and also able to load custom wavetables. Capable of linear, cubic, quadratic, lagrange, or sinc interpolation. Also capable of [Oversampling](Oversampling.md).

- Pure tones can be generated without oversampling or sinc interpolation.
- When doing extreme modulation, best practice is to use sinc interpolation and an oversampling index of 1 (2x).
- Try all the combinations of interpolation and oversampling to find the best tradeoff between quality and CPU usage for your application.

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.summary: Args:     world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

args.description: Frequency of the oscillator in Hz.
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is 0.0).
args.name: trig

args.description: Type of waveform. See the OscType struct for options (default is OscType.sine). Best if provided as OscType.sine, OscType.triangle, etc.
args.name: osc_type

overloads.name: next

returns.doc: The next sample of the oscillator output.

overloads.signature: next(mut self: Osc[num_chans, interp, os_index], freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: Bool = False, osc_type: SIMD[DType.index, num_chans] = OscType.sine) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Generate the next oscillator sample on a single waveform type. All inputs are SIMD types except trig, which is a scalar. This means that an oscillator can have num_chans different instances, each with its own frequency, phase offset, and waveform type, but they will all share the same trigger signal.

overloads.name: next_all_basic_waveforms
overloads.signature: next_all_basic_waveforms(mut self, freq: Float64 = 100, phase: Float64 = 0, last_phase: Float64 = 0, trig: Bool = False) -> SIMD[DType.float64, 4]
overloads.summary: Returns the next sample of all basic waveforms (sine, triangle, saw, square) in a SIMD vector, where each waveform is in a different lane.

args.description: Frequency of the oscillator in Hz.
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is 0.0).
args.name: trig

args.description: List of waveform types ([OscType](MMMWorld.md/#struct-osctype)) to interpolate between (default is [OscType.sine,OscType.triangle,OscType.saw,OscType.square].
args.name: osc_types

args.description: Fractional index for wavetable interpolation. Values are between 0.0 and 1.0. 0.0 corresponds to the first waveform in the osc_types list, 1.0 corresponds to the last waveform in the osc_types list, and values in between interpolate linearly between all waveforms in the list.
args.name: osc_frac

overloads.name: next_basic_waveforms

returns.doc: The next sample of the oscillator output.

overloads.signature: next_basic_waveforms(mut self, freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: Bool = False, osc_types: List[Int] = List[Int](OscType.sine, OscType.triangle, OscType.saw, OscType.square, Tuple[]()), osc_frac: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Variable Wavetable Oscillator using built-in waveforms. Generates the next oscillator sample on a variable  waveform where the output is interpolated between  different waveform types. All inputs are SIMD types except trig and osc_types, which are scalar. This  means that an oscillator can have num_chans different instances, each with its own frequency, phase offset,  and waveform type, but they will all share the same trigger signal and the same list of waveform types  to interpolate between.

args.description: Reference to a Buffer containing the waveforms to interpolate between.
args.name: buffer

args.description: Frequency of the oscillator in Hz.
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is 0.0). All waveforms will reset together.
args.name: trig

args.description: Fractional index for wavetable interpolation. Values are between 0.0 and 1.0. 0.0 corresponds to the first channel in the input buffer, 1.0 corresponds to the last channel in the input buffer, and values in between interpolate linearly between all channels in the buffer.
args.name: osc_frac

overloads.name: next_vwt
overloads.signature: next_vwt(mut self: Osc[num_chans, interp, os_index], ref buffer: Buffer, freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: Bool = False, osc_frac: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Variable Wavetable Oscillator that interpolates over a loaded Buffer. Generates the next oscillator sample on a variable waveform where the output is interpolated between  different different channels of a provided Buffer.

args.description: Reference to a Buffer containing the waveforms to interpolate between.
args.name: buffer

args.description: Frequency of the oscillator in Hz.
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is 0.0). All waveforms will reset together.
args.name: trig

args.description: Fractional index for wavetable interpolation. Values are between 0.0 and 1.0. 0.0 corresponds to the first channel in the input buffer, 1.0 corresponds to the last channel in the input buffer, and values in between interpolate linearly between all channels in the buffer.
args.name: osc_frac

overloads.name: next_vwt
overloads.signature: next_vwt[simd_chans: Int](mut self: Osc[num_chans, interp, os_index], ref buffer: SIMDBuffer[simd_chans], freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: Bool = False, osc_frac: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Variable Wavetable Oscillator that interpolates over a loaded SIMDBuffer. Generates the next oscillator sample on a variable waveform where the output is interpolated between  different different channels of a provided Buffer. This should only be used with low channel counts (maybe up to 4 or 8 channels depending on the CPU).

structs.name: Osc

parameters.description: Interpolation method. See [Interp](MMMWorld.md/#struct-interp) struct for options (default is Interp.linear).
parameters.name: interp

parameters.description: [Oversampling](Oversampling.md) index (0 = no oversampling, 1 = 2x, 2 = 4x, etc.; default is 0).
parameters.name: os_index

structs.signature: struct Osc[num_chans: Int = 1, interp: Int = Interp.linear, os_index: Int = 0]

structs.description:
This oscillator generates a non-bandlimited sawtooth waveform. It is useful for modulation, but should be avoided for audio-rate synthesis due to comptimeing.

Outputs values between 0.0 and 1.0.

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.summary: Args:     world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

args.description: Frequency of the sawtooth wave in Hz.
args.name: freq

args.description: Offsets the phase of the oscillator (default is 0.0).
args.name: phase_offset

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True (default is 0.0).
args.name: trig

overloads.name: next
overloads.signature: next(mut self: LFSaw[num_chans], freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 100, phase_offset: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0, trig: Bool = False) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Generate the next sawtooth wave sample.

modulation, but should be avoided for audio-rate synthesis due to comptimeing.

structs.name: LFTri

structs.description: Dust has a Phasor as its core, and the frequency of the Phasor is randomly changed each time an impulse is generated. This allows the Dust to be used in multiple ways. It can be used as a simple random impulse generator, or the user can use the get_phase() method to get the current phase of the internal Phasor and use that phase to drive other oscillators or processes. The user can also set the phase of the internal Phasor using the set_phase() method, allowing for more complex interactions.

overloads.summary: Args:     world: Pointer to the MMMWorld instance.

args.name: low

args.description: Upper bound for the random frequency range.
args.name: high

args.description: Trigger signal to reset the phase when switching from False to True.
args.name: trig

overloads.summary: Generate the next dust noise sample as a boolean impulse.
